L1.area: API request processing and upstream data repository interaction logic

L2.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in an API endpoint handler where, when an upstream data repository call fails and returns a non-nil error alongside a nil problem details struct, the error-handling branch incorrectly accesses a field on the nil problem details variable instead of the locally constructed error response struct. This causes a runtime panic and HTTP 500 response, resulting in per-request denial of service whenever the upstream service is unavailable.

L3.area: PFD (Packet Flow Description) management PATCH handler in the SBI processor layer
L3.description: A nil pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the PFD management PATCH endpoint handler where, when an upstream UDR (Unified Data Repository) call fails and returns a non-nil error alongside a nil problem details struct, the error-handling branch incorrectly accesses a field on the nil problem details variable instead of the locally constructed error response struct. This causes a runtime panic and HTTP 500 response when the upstream UDR is unavailable.

ground_truth.reason: PatchIndividualApplicationPFDManagement reads problemDetails.Cause in the errPfdData != nil branch where problemDetails is nil by construction (the prior case already handled the non-nil case), causing a nil pointer dereference panic.
